behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at the real STT API. If the API doesn't return speaker labels or returns them in an unexpected format, a mocked STT would hide this. Testing with a real STT API call is necessary to verify that speaker labels are both returned and correctly handled through the service pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the service with a real audio file containing multiple speakers. Verify that the STT API returns speaker labels, the service correctly extracts them from the API response, and the labels are preserved in the output so that statements can be attributed to specific attendees.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the LLM's ability to extract and correctly attribute action items to attendees. The model might fail to recognize assignments, misattribute them, or omit some attendees from the action items list. This is model-judgment quality that requires testing with a real LLM; mocking would hide these failures.
behaviors.test_description: Transcribe a meeting with multiple attendees and clear task assignments. Call the real LLM with the transcript and a prompt to produce a structured summary. Verify that every attendee who was assigned a task in the meeting appears in the action items section with at least one item attributed to them.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the LLM's ability to correctly categorize decisions and place them in the appropriate structured section. A mocked LLM could always place decisions correctly, hiding the real failure where the actual model embeds decisions in the narrative or fails to extract them as discrete items.
behaviors.test_description: Transcribe a meeting with clearly stated key decisions (e.g., 'We decided to adopt Python as our backend language'). Call the real LLM to produce a structured summary with separate "decisions" and narrative sections. Verify that major decisions appear in the decisions section and are not only mentioned in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur at multiple boundaries: (1) the service's orchestration and splitting logic, (2) the STT API's handling of individual segments, and (3) the LLM's ability to merge segment transcripts coherently. Testing at Integration with mocks misses real API failures. Testing at System (real STT, mocked LLM) or Agentic (real LLM, mocked STT) alone misses cross-boundary failures. Workflow level with both real STT and real LLM calls is required to catch failures in orchestration, API integration, and model coherence.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a 90-minute meeting recording to the service. Verify that the service splits it into segments (e.g., 60 min + 30 min), calls the real STT API for each segment independently, passes the segment transcripts to the real LLM with a merge/coherence prompt, and returns a single coherent summary that accurately represents the entire meeting without gaps, contradictions, or loss of key information.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Real failures span all infrastructure levels: file I/O (System), STT API integration (System), LLM API integration (Agentic), and orchestration (Workflow). The most likely real failure is an orchestration bug or API issue that causes the pipeline to hang, timeout, or crash mid-process. Testing at Integration with mocks would miss real API timeouts, unexpected response formats, rate-limiting, and LLM quality issues. Workflow level with real file I/O and live API calls is necessary to verify the pipeline completes end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting recording (10–60 minutes with multiple speakers) to the service. Monitor execution through all steps: upload, transcription, LLM summarization, and output delivery. Verify that the service completes the full pipeline automatically without user intervention, timeouts, or partial failures, and that structured output (action items, decisions, attendee attributions) is successfully delivered.
